description_long: This is a rugged hammer with a metallic claw head and a robust, cylindrical handle. The head features a dual-purpose design: one side is a flat striking face, while the other is a curved claw for prying nails. The handle appears to be made of wood or a composite material, reinforced with two pinkish metal bands and a blue cylindrical insert, possibly for added strength or shock absorption. The surface of the handle shows a textured, slightly worn finish, suggesting frequent use. The hammer also has a black cord or wire attached to the base of the handle, possibly for hanging or securing the tool. The overall appearance is utilitarian, with visible signs of wear and a mix of metallic and wooden textures.
description: A well-used claw hammer with a metal head, wooden handle, and reinforcing bands, featuring a black cord at the base. The tool shows a mix of metallic and wood textures with visible wear.
description_view_0: The hammer is seen from an angled top view, highlighting the metallic head and the reinforcing bands along the handle.
description_view_1: A clear side profile shows the full length of the handle, the dual-sided head with claw and striking face, and the attached black cord.
description_view_2: This view emphasizes the back of the hammer, showing the curvature of the claw and the cylindrical handle with its reinforcing bands.
description_view_3: A direct side view displays the entire hammer, including the head, handle, reinforcing bands, and the black cord at the base.
attribute_handle_type: Straight wooden handle with a textured, slightly worn finish, reinforced by two pinkish metal bands and a blue cylindrical insert, and a black cord at the base.
attribute_head_type: Claw hammer head made of steel, featuring a flat striking face on one side and a curved claw for prying on the other.
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: Visible wear and surface abrasions on the handle and head, with some discoloration and minor scratches.
attribute_attachment_or_accessory: Black cord attached to the base of the handle
